mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower FEDFUNDS directly compresses required returns, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.41% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk. This triggers a flight-to-safety response, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.1% provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust aggregate demand and earnings growth expectations. The joint acceleration of both demand indicators produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as consensus upgrades earnings forecasts.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492003M and INDPRO at 107.24 suggest moderate demand conditions
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant difference between high-interaction and low-interaction regimes (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over 2 months signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward in response to deteriorating production capacity utilization.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.24 provides baseline for decline detection
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal construction and labor market momentum. When both series accelerate simultaneously, it indicates strong forward-looking demand and capital investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as GDP growth expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1192K and PAYEMS at 147810K indicate moderate labor and housing activity
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no material difference between synchronized and desynchronized labor-housing regimes (t-test p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, it signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate future rate hikes and duration risk.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 249.25 and FEDFUNDS at 1.41% define current monetary regime
regime_hint: inflation acceleration with accommodative policy lag
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in this regime are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.06).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50K new jobs per month over a 2-month rolling average, signaling labor market softening. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability and reduce equity allocations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147810K provides baseline for deceleration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite labor market deceleration signal, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter remains above 2% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) show month-over-month growth above 0.3%. This synchronized demand expansion produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate and equity risk premiums compress.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized > 2.0% AND RSAFS MoM > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17272.5B and RSAFS at 492003M indicate moderate growth conditions
regime_hint: sustained mid-to-late cycle expansion with synchronized demand
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in synchronized growth regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.07).
